fields.description:
## Why
One combined-tree CI run must satisfy Gerrit's PER-CHANGE `Verified` gate. The bot casts `Verified` on the batch members from that single run — sound because ancestor-atomic submit means the untested intermediate states never reach `main`. This is the only gate-touching change in the epic and needs an explicit security audit.

## What
After the combined-tip CI passes, the bot (Service User `autolander-bot`) casts `Verified +1` on each batch member, then ancestor-atomic submits the whole batch. **S8 owns the cast-Verified → submit step** (S7 only assembled the chain and triggered the one CI run).

## Dependencies
Depends on **S7** (`arthrodial-skipping-gypsymoth`, 0128-9a17-79db-450e) — the assembled combined chain + the single combined-tip CI run: S8 casts per-member `Verified` from *that* run and then submits, so the assembly + one-run trigger must exist first.

## Scope
Phase 2. The Verified-casting mechanic, the atomic submit, + its security audit. Depends on the batching-assembly story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **SHA-binding (new threat vector):** at assembly the `{change_id → patchset_SHA}` map is **written durably to the autolander state dir** (a file that survives crash/restart), not held only in memory; before casting `Verified +1` on a member the bot **reads it back** and asserts that member's stored SHA is exactly the one included in the combined-tip run that passed. On crash/restart the batch is re-validated against the stored map before any vote is cast. (GerriScary/copyCondition guards *carried* votes; SHA-binding guards *directly-cast* votes — a distinct vector.) Never a copied or stale vote.
- [ ] **Atomicity mechanism:** submit the whole batch atomically via `submitWholeTopic=true` (already deployed) — the bot sets a shared topic across all members of the single linear relation chain before submit; ancestor-atomic submit means intermediate states never reach `main`. (Parallel-sibling topologies are excluded from a batch; ADR-0040.)
- [ ] **Run-outcome confirmation:** the bot learns the combined-tip run outcome from the `Verified` vote the gerrit-to-platform bridge casts on the tip change (the existing per-patchset path) AND asserts the observed GHA `run_id` matches the one S7 dispatched for this batch (from the handoff record) — so it never fans out votes off a stale or mismatched run.
- [ ] **Fan-out protocol + mid-run failure:** only after the confirmed combined-tip run passes, **loop `ssh gerrit review --verified +1 <change>,<patchset>` over the members in chain order**, each targeting the post-assembly patchset; on **any** per-member vote failure, **abort — cast no submit** — and hand the batch back via S9. If the run itself fails, cast none and hand the batch to the re-batch path (S9).
- [ ] `Verified`-casting is restricted to the bot Service User ACL; documented that per-change `Verified` now means "tested as part of its landed batch," not "tested in isolation."
- [ ] **Security-invariants artifact:** the ADR-0042 (created by S6 `broken-checkered-tiger`) Security invariants section records the SHA-binding invariant (cast-Verified bound to the tested combined-tip SHA) and its concrete check — a test asserting the SHA-binding logic rejects a member whose SHA is not in the passed run — alongside the soundness argument (atomic submit ⇒ intermediate states never reach `main`), the no-copied-vote path, and the bot-only ACL; the two-vote gate is not weakened.
- [ ] The `submitWholeTopic` / runbook / CONTRIBUTING.md doc-drift from this semantic shift is handed to **S6's** docs sweep.
